title: podman machine camera support
body:
### Feature request description

I want to use my built in webcam(or another webcam) in podman machine

### Suggest potential solution

I would like to be able to run a container in podman machine that can see /dev/video0 as the webcam on that laptop.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

not aware of any alternatives

### Additional context

created https://github.com/coreos/fedora-coreos-tracker/issues/1928 as a place to track any camera drivers that may need to be added.

comments.body: hi @jtligon as far a drivers go, it might be best to consider https://github.com/containers/podman-machine-os ... it might be easier to get things in and won't impact all of fcos unless that is your idea.  besides, on that repo, i know a guy ...

What exactly is the actual use case? IMO generic device pass through is out of scope for podman machine.
We have to support 5 different hypervisors and doing that sounds like a ton of work.

As such I rather not go down that route.

I have a camera looking at my wife's kiln in the garage. I'd like to debug that container on my laptop. 

If we reduced scope to just applehv or krunkit, would that make it more or less tedious?

> If we reduced scope to just applehv or krunkit, would that make it more or less tedious?

Sure code wise yes, but generally I am not the biggest fan of that. Documentation then needs to be very explicit that this only supported one that one thing and users then likely ask to support it on other platforms as well sooner or later. So while it would help initially I don't see that log term as better.

There is also the question on what camera exactly, built into the laptop, external one via USB, some PCI capture card, etc.. Hardware might be vastly different and could need different ways to pass into the VM.

And then this is something that cannot be tested automatically in CI because we simply will have no machines with such devices which means it can break any time without us noticing.

All very fantastic points for why it's hard and annoying. 

I completely see your point of view here.

@jtligon in talking with the vfkit folks, it seems that usb-passthrough is not even supported there for things like this.  If you want this to work, even outside podman, i think it would need to be 1) supported by applehv 2)wired into vfkit ... and so on.  I do think long term we are going to see more of this but rn hypervisors seems the be the limitation.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
